text:
In the sleepy little town of Willowbrook, lived an ingenious inventor named Herbert Jenkins. Every year since he could remember, Herbert would spend his weekends modifying old automobiles into quirky little beasts. This year was different though; this would be his most ambitious project yet.

He decided to build a car that could park itself. How trivial it seemed at first. Park a car.

But no. Park itself.

He spent months on research and design. He imagined a car that knew the lay of the land like an old paladin. It would use sensors to plot the perfect parking spots. On rainy days it could transform into a gentle giant. On holidays, it could turn into a festive bird-themed chariot. Herbert even dubbed this masterpiece the *Auto-Park*.

The day finally arrived. Herbert's front yard was awash with birds looking rather perplexed as they pecked at his freshly painted *Auto-Park*. They pecked at the letters "AP." Herbert laughed. Maybe he
